officialSources.title: Create, view or download a file
evidence: Google documents automatic online saving for Docs, Sheets, Slides and Forms.
Google documents offline access that saves changes to the device and saves them to Drive once reconnected.

officialSources.title: Use Google Drive files offline
evidence: Google documents offline access for Google Docs, Sheets and Slides.
Google documents browser, extension, sign-in, and device-space prerequisites for offline editing.

officialSources.title: Find out what's changed in a file
evidence: Google documents version history for Docs, Sheets, Slides and Vids.
Google documents that browsing earlier versions requires edit permission.

officialSources.title: Troubleshoot Google Sheets can't save your changes
evidence: Google documents a Sheets-specific save failure where users must copy recent edits, reload, and paste.
Google documents offline mode as a mitigation that locally saves work and uploads changes when a stable connection returns.

officialSources.title: Fix common issues in Google Drive
evidence: Google documents Drive connection, temporary error, browser, cache, file-size, and offline-access troubleshooting.
Google points users to the Google Workspace Status Dashboard when there is a known Drive or server outage.

officialSources.title: Check the status of a Google Workspace service
evidence: Google documents a Workspace Status Dashboard for current and past core service status.
Google documents incident details, estimated resolution, final updates, and RSS or JSON subscriptions.

officialSources.title: Save, back up, and recover a file in Microsoft Office
evidence: Microsoft documents OneDrive or SharePoint storage as enabling AutoSave and Version History.
Microsoft documents those features as helping prevent data loss when a file closes unexpectedly.

officialSources.title: What you should know about AutoSave
evidence: Microsoft documents AutoSave for files stored in OneDrive, OneDrive for work or school, or SharePoint in Microsoft 365.
Microsoft documents Version History as the recovery path for accidental changes.

officialSources.title: View previous versions of Office files
evidence: Microsoft documents previous-version browsing for Office files stored in OneDrive or SharePoint.
Microsoft documents comparing versions and restoring the desired version.

officialSources.title: Collaborate on Excel workbooks at the same time with co-authoring
evidence: Microsoft documents Excel co-authoring requirements, supported versions, cloud storage requirements, and AutoSave dependency.
Microsoft documents that offline OneDrive changes are merged when the computer comes back online.

officialSources.title: Document collaboration and co-authoring
evidence: Microsoft documents sync-folder behavior where files sync when users come back online.
Microsoft documents conflict alerts when offline edits conflict with cloud changes.

officialSources.title: Fix OneDrive sync problems
evidence: Microsoft documents OneDrive sync error states and troubleshooting for Windows, macOS, and file explorer integrations.
Microsoft documents that storage-limit problems can block uploading, editing, or syncing new files.

officialSources.title: How to check Microsoft 365 service health
evidence: Microsoft documents the Microsoft 365 admin center Service health page for current Microsoft service health.
Microsoft documents that service health includes affected scenarios, impact, start time, and next update information.

limitations: This artifact captures current official public documentation, not a live tenant or desktop-client failure injection run against Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel.
The comparison proves direct external reliability evidence exists; it does not claim bilig is 10x better across reliability or every objective category.

dimensions.title: Autosave And Version Recovery
biligEvidence: The reliability scorecard exercises Zero-backed browser persistence, submitted mutation acknowledgment, failed retry state, and authoritative reconnect/rebase behavior.
The auditability scorecard separately proves preview/apply, undo, revert, and headed-browser change review flows.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents automatic saving while online, offline device saving, and Drive save-on-reconnect behavior.
Google documents version history for Sheets and related editors.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents AutoSave and Version History for Excel files stored in OneDrive or SharePoint.
Microsoft documents previous-version browse, compare, and restore flows for Office files.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has executable local durability and replay controls; Google Sheets and Excel rely on documented cloud autosave, offline save, and version-history systems.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs a production disaster-recovery and retention policy artifact before this dimension can become a broad reliability lead claim.
Google and Microsoft tenant settings, client versions, and storage location materially change incumbent recovery behavior.

dimensions.title: Offline Local Edit Durability
biligEvidence: The offline-network-partition control simulates 32 offline edits across worker reload, remote authoritative edits on reconnect, local mutation acknowledgments, and final reload.
The headed-browser reload and crash-soak controls verify persisted workbook state survives full reload and crash/restart paths.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents offline Docs, Sheets and Slides use with explicit browser, extension, sign-in, and available-space prerequisites.
Google documents a Sheets save-failure path where offline mode locally saves work and uploads after stable reconnection.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents OneDrive-backed offline edits merging when the computer returns online.
Microsoft documents sync-folder files syncing on reconnect and surfacing alerts when conflicts occur.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig's checked reliability artifact directly exercises offline pending-operation replay; Sheets and Excel document offline availability with browser/client and cloud-sync prerequisites.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs a headed browser offline soak against the production server path before claiming end-user dominance.
The external incumbent evidence is official documentation, not a live benchmark under identical network partitions.

dimensions.title: Collaborative Sync Conflict Recovery
biligEvidence: The authoritative-rebase control applies an unrelated authoritative event over an unsent local mutation and verifies both authoritative and local cells survive reload.
The submitted-ack and failed-retry controls verify submitted mutations are absorbed on authoritative acknowledgment and failed mutations survive reload for retry.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents a Sheets path where users copy edits, reload, and paste when the server takes too long to save changes.
Google documents Drive troubleshooting for connection, temporary server, browser, cache, file-size, and offline-access issues.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents file-locked and visibility issues when versions or settings do not support Excel co-authoring.
Microsoft documents OneDrive sync errors, storage-limit failures, conflict alerts, and sync troubleshooting.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig's local-first model has executable rebase and retry controls; Google Sheets and Excel expose documented user-facing recovery workflows for save, sync, and co-authoring conflicts.
residualLimitations: bilig still needs direct multi-actor user-facing conflict-resolution UX scoring against Sheets and Excel.
The official docs comparison does not measure real-world conflict frequency.

dimensions.title: Service Health And Error Recovery
biligEvidence: The reliability scorecard verifies local reload, crash/restart, retry, rebase, and offline partition controls as executable repo gates.
The current bilig evidence does not yet include a public hosted service status dashboard or incident feed artifact.
googleSheetsEvidence: Google documents Drive and server outage checks through the Google Workspace Status Dashboard.
Google documents Drive troubleshooting guidance for temporary errors and weak or unavailable connections.
microsoftExcelEvidence: Microsoft documents the Microsoft 365 admin Service health page with incident details and next update information.
Microsoft documents OneDrive sync status and error troubleshooting for local sync clients.
dimensions.comparisonResult: bilig has stronger repo-executable local failure controls than before, but Google and Microsoft have broader documented service-health operations surfaces.
residualLimitations: This dimension remains a product operations gap for overall reliability dominance.
Future work should add bilig service-health and incident-evidence artifacts or explicitly scope the product away from hosted service obligations.
